title: Phaseolus metcalfei
taxonomy: Phaseolus metcalfei is a species of wild bean native to Central America. It belongs to the subgenus Phaseolus, which includes various species of beans. As a member of the subgenus Phaseolus, Phaseolus metcalfei is further classified within the genus Phaseolus, which is part of the legume family (Fabaceae). The classification of Phaseolus metcalfei is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Fabales, Family: Fabaceae, Genus: Phaseolus, Species: P. metcalfei. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships among different species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Phaseolus metcalfei is a species of wild bean native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of Phaseolus metcalfei include isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phaseolus metcalfei is often used to make teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into supplement capsules and tinctures to support cardiovascular health and well-being.
